# Circle USDC Transparency & Reserves: Substantive Policy Documentation

## Reserve Composition & Backing Policy

**Core Commitment (Verbatim):**
> "USDC is always redeemable 1:1 for US dollars, and EURC is always redeemable 1:1 for euros. Always."

**Reserve Composition (Verbatim):**
> "USDC is a digital dollar backed 100% by highly liquid cash and cash-equivalent assets and is always redeemable 1:1 for US dollars."

**Specific Asset Classes Held:**

The reserves comprise three primary categories:
1. Cash deposits at leading financial institutions
2. US Treasury securities (short-dated)
3. Overnight US Treasury repurchase agreements

**Circle Reserve Fund (USDXX):**
> "The majority of the USDC reserve is held in the Circle Reserve Fund (USDXX), an SEC-registered 2a-7 government money market fund."

> "The Circle Reserve Fund can contain cash, short-dated US Treasuries and overnight US Treasury repurchase agreements with leading global banks. These are commonly used assets in money market funds because of their liquidity and stability."

**Remainder Allocation (Verbatim):**
> "The remainder of the reserve is held in cash, mostly among a handful of the world's largest banks with the highest capital, liquidity and supervisory requirements in the world."

---

## Attestation & Audit Regime

**Frequency & Third-Party Assurance (Verbatim):**
> "USDC reserve holdings are fully disclosed on a weekly basis, along with associated mint/burn flows. Additionally, a Big Four accounting firm provides monthly third-party assurance that the value of USDC reserves are greater than the amount of USDC in circulation."

**Attestation Standards (Verbatim):**
> "The reports are prepared according to attestation standards set out by the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA)."

**Historical Scope (Verbatim):**
> "we've issued reports on all reserve assets since 2018, along with SEC filings in 2021 and 2022."

**Current Independent Auditor (Verbatim):**
> "Deloitte & Touche LLP is Circle's independent auditor and has audited Circle's financials since fiscal 2022. Prior to Deloitte, Grant Thornton LLP served as Circle's independent auditor from 2015."

---

## Named Reserve Custodians & Banking Partners

**BlackRock – Circle Reserve Fund Manager:**

Named as manager of USDXX (the SEC-registered 2a-7 money market fund holding majority of USDC reserves). The page states:

> "Daily, independent, third-party reporting on the portfolio is publicly available via BlackRock."

Specific URL referenced: BlackRock USDXX product page — https://www.blackrock.com/cash/en-us/products/329365/

**Leading Global Financial Institutions (Generic Reference):**

The page does not name specific depository banks beyond describing them generically as:
> "a handful of the world's largest banks with the highest capital, liquidity and supervisory requirements in the world."

No specific custodian names (e.g., BNY Mellon) appear on this transparency page.

---

## Structural Disclosure & Banking Infrastructure Process

**Weekly + Monthly Cadence (Verbatim):**
> "USDC reserve holdings are fully disclosed on a weekly basis, along with associated mint/burn flows."

> "a Big Four accounting firm provides monthly third-party assurance that the value of USDC reserves are greater than the amount of USDC in circulation."

**Scale of Banking Integration (Verbatim):**
> "During the 12 months ending on October 12, 2023, Circle bridged more than $277 billion between the banking system and blockchains through minting and redeeming USDC."

**Ongoing Infrastructure Development (Verbatim):**
> "We are continually strengthening our global banking infrastructure to facilitate local creation and redemption of our stablecoins, and to ensure USDC stability."

**Reserve Design Purpose (Verbatim):**
> "The reserve is designed to provide holders with ready liquidity, even under extremely stressed conditions."

---

## Regulatory Framework & Regulatory Status

**SEC Oversight:**
Circle identifies itself as operating an "SEC-registered 2a-7 government money market fund" (the USDXX vehicle). The page also references:
> "SEC filings in 2021 and 2022"

**NYDFS / State Money Transmitter Licensing (from footer):**
> "Circle Internet Financial, LLC is licensed as a Money Transmitter by the New York State Department of Financial Institutions and to engage in Virtual Currency Business Activity by the New York State Department of Financial Services."

**Bermuda Regulatory Status (from footer):**
> "Circle International Bermuda Limited is licensed to conduct digital asset business by the Bermuda Monetary Authority."

**No MiCA Mention:**
MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation, EU) is not explicitly mentioned on this transparency page.

---

## Transparency as Core Value (Verbatim Language)

**Commitment Statement (Verbatim):**
> "As part of our strong commitment to transparency, we've issued reports on all reserve assets since 2018, along with SEC filings in 2021 and 2022."

---

## Attestation Report URLs & Archive Structure

Reports are organized by year and month. **Active/available reports include:**

**USDC Attestation Reports (2018–present):**
- Reports available from October 2018 onward via HubSpot CDN: `https://6778953.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/6778953/USDCAttestationReports/`
- Pattern: `[YEAR]/[YEAR] USDC_Examination Report [MONTH] [YY].pdf`
- Example: January 2026 report: `https://6778953.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/6778953/USDCAttestationReports/2026/2026%20USDC_Examination%20Report%20January%2026.pdf`

**EURC Attestation Reports (2023–present):**
- Path: `https://6778953.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/6778953/EURC%20Attestations/`
- First reports: June 2022 (labeled EUROC); renamed to EURC from 2023 onward
- Pattern: `[YEAR] EURC_Examination Report [MONTH] [YY].pdf`
- Example: January 2026: `https://6778953.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/6778953/EURC%20Attestations/2026%20EURC_Examination%20Report%20January%2026.pdf`

**Reports marked as not-yet-available** (future months in 2026) redirect to `#` (no-op anchor).

---

## Issuance & Redemption Disclosure

The page displays **7-day, 30-day, and 365-day issuance/redemption summaries** updated as of April 23, 2026, but specific dollar amounts are not substantive policy and are omitted per Path A scope.
